summary: Deprecated audit trail for the VS Code Git autofetch implementation observation. Visual Studio Code documents the git.autofetch setting, but this registry path is not a published Microsoft control surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Another option is to disable the auto fetch feature by changing the following setting: "git.autofetch": false.
validation_proof.notes: The page proves the git.autofetch setting exists, but it does not prove the app's Explorer\\Advanced\\DisableGitAutofetch registry path.

setting.name: VS Code Git Autofetch
setting.area: VS Code Setting vs Registry Observation
setting.casual_explanation: VS Code can automatically fetch Git changes in the background. Some developers like that convenience, while others prefer to turn it off to reduce background activity.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable autofetch when they want less background network activity, fewer automatic Git refreshes, or more predictable manual control of repository state.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning autofetch off can reduce background activity, but the official VS Code control is a settings.json option, not the Explorer registry value the current app writes.
setting.professional_notes: VS Code documents git.autofetch in its settings reference. The current app writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\DisableGitAutofetch = 1 instead. This research pass did not capture a VS Code source that publishes that registry value as the supported control surface.

allowed_values.label: VS Code default autofetch behavior
allowed_values.meaning: VS Code uses its normal default behavior when git.autofetch is not explicitly set in settings.

allowed_values.label: Autofetch disabled
allowed_values.meaning: VS Code does not automatically fetch Git changes in the background.

allowed_values.label: Autofetch enabled
allowed_values.meaning: VS Code automatically fetches Git changes in the background.

allowed_values.label: VS Code registry mapping not captured
allowed_values.meaning: This research pass did not capture a primary VS Code source that publishes DisableGitAutofetch as the supported control surface.

allowed_values.label: Observed app profile
allowed_values.meaning: The current app writes 1 here to represent disabled Git autofetch, but that registry value is not validated as the official VS Code control.

targets.notes: Tracked as an observed app write only.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app targets the right feature area, but it writes an Explorer registry value instead of the documented VS Code settings surface.

windows_defaults.label: VS Code settings default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Users who have not explicitly set git.autofetch in VS Code settings

states.rationale: Keep the official VS Code setting unset until the app implementation is aligned.
Do not publish the observed registry write as the official VS Code control.

windows_defaults.notes: This record separates the documented VS Code setting from the current registry-based app implementation.

recommended_profiles.label: Hold for research

states.rationale: Keep the public record conservative until the app targets the documented VS Code setting.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to document the gap between the product and the documented VS Code setting.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need the app implementation itself to already target the documented VS Code setting.
tradeoffs: The official VS Code setting is preserved in the dataset.
The current app write remains blocked from validated publication.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app profile

states.rationale: Observed registry value written by the current app.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting the app's current behavior for later implementation cleanup.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a sourced VS Code settings implementation.
tradeoffs: The current behavior does not use the documented VS Code settings surface.
recommended_profiles.notes: Blocked until the app writes the documented VS Code setting instead of the registry value.

evidence.title: VS Code documentation: Default settings reference
evidence.summary: VS Code documents git.autofetch in its settings reference as the supported control surface for background Git autofetch behavior.

evidence.title: VS Code documentation: Source Control FAQ
evidence.summary: The FAQ explicitly says the auto fetch feature can be disabled with the git.autofetch setting.

decision.why: VS Code's git.autofetch setting is validated at the feature level, but the app's Explorer\\Advanced\\DisableGitAutofetch registry path is still not validated by a primary source.
blocking_issues: VS Code'un git.autofetch ayarı doğrulandı ancak uygulamanın yazdığı Explorer\Advanced\DisableGitAutofetch registry path'i için birincil Microsoft kaynağı bulunamadı.
The app writes an Explorer registry value instead of the documented git.autofetch setting.
